description: Fleet-quota escrow (ADR-7) client decision semantics. Pins the allow/block decision at each governed call and the consumption report so the escrow client is behavior-identical in the TypeScript SDK (sdk/src/governance/escrow.ts, driven by sdk/tests/unit/escrow-conformance.test.ts) and the Python SDK (sdk-python/obsvr/escrow.py, driven by sdk-python/tests/test_escrow_conformance.py).
authoritative_source: This repo. The client decision cases here must stay identical across TS and Python.

ops.grant: apply one escrow grant for rule_id with {share, epoch}; a non-strictly-increasing epoch is ignored (stale/replayed)
ops.poll_response: apply the full quota_escrow map from a /policies response (escrow: object of grants, or null = absent field => clear all escrow); rules present are granted, rules absent lose escrow
ops.spend: consume one unit of rule_id's local share; expect {escrow, allowed, remaining}
ops.peek: check-only (shadow/explain) read of rule_id's share, no consumption; expect {escrow, allowed, remaining}
ops.has_escrow: whether escrow is in effect for rule_id; expect bool
ops.report: snapshot quota_consumed for the next poll; expect {rule_id: {consumed, epoch}}

cases.desc: A grant is spent one unit per call; the call after exhaustion blocks (quota_exceeded); the report tallies units actually consumed under the grant's epoch.
A fresh grant (new epoch) refills remaining and resets the per-epoch consumption counter; the next report is tagged with the new epoch.

When the global budget is fully committed the allocator grants share 0; the instance blocks immediately (fail-closed) rather than overspending, and reports zero consumption so the whole grant is reclaimable.
On a poll failure no fresh grant arrives; the instance spends only its residual share and then blocks — it never fabricates share. The eventual successful poll reports what it used.
A poll_response grants shares for several rules at once; each rule's share depletes independently and both are reported.

A rule absent from quota_escrow has no escrow in effect (spend signals escrow=false so the caller uses the per-process meter). An absent quota_escrow field (null) clears all escrow — backward compatible with servers that never send it.

A grant whose epoch is not strictly greater than the current epoch (lower OR equal) is ignored, so a reordered or duplicated response can never resurrect a spent share; only a strictly newer epoch refills.
After a rule's share is exhausted and blocking, a later poll_response with a strictly newer epoch refills the share and unblocks; consumption is reported under the new epoch.
